metadata.title: WHO good manufacturing practices for biological products
metadata.document_version: WHO Technical Report Series, No. 999, Annex 2 (66th report of WHO Expert Committee on Biological Standardization) — replaces Annex 1 of WHO TRS No. 822 (1992)
metadata.note: No prior extraction found in output-v2/v3/v4. Full extraction performed. This document covers GMP for biological products for human use — scope includes vaccines, mAbs, ATMPs, allergens, fermentation products. Sections: scope, PQS/QRM, personnel, starting materials, seed lots/cell banks, premises, containment, clean rooms, production, campaign production, labelling, validation, quality control, documentation, use of animals.

rules.rule_text: Biological products must be manufactured in accordance with the requirements of a pharmaceutical quality system (PQS) based on a lifecycle approach. QRM principles must be used to develop the control strategy across all manufacturing and control stages, including materials sourcing and storage, personnel and materials flow, manufacture and packaging, quality control, quality assurance, storage and distribution.
rules.source_section: Section 5 — Pharmaceutical quality system and quality risk management
rules.verbatim_quote: Biological products, like any pharmaceutical product, should be manufactured in accordance with the requirements of a pharmaceutical quality system (PQS) based on a life-cycle approach as defined in WHO good manufacturing practices for pharmaceutical products: main principles (2)... QRM principles should be used to develop the control strategy across all manufacturing and control stages – including materials sourcing and storage, personnel and materials flow, manufacture and packaging, quality control, quality assurance, storage and distribution activities.
cross_references: WHO GMP for pharmaceutical products TRS 986 Annex 2

rules.rule_text: Personnel responsible for production and quality control of biological products must have an adequate background in relevant scientific disciplines including microbiology, biology, biometry, chemistry, medicine, pharmacy, pharmacology, virology, immunology, biotechnology and veterinary medicine, together with sufficient practical experience.
rules.verbatim_quote: Personnel responsible for production and control should have an adequate background in relevant scientific disciplines such as microbiology, biology, biometry, chemistry, medicine, pharmacy, pharmacology, virology, immunology, biotechnology and veterinary medicine, together with sufficient practical experience to enable them to perform their duties.

rules.rule_text: The health status of personnel engaged in production, maintenance, testing and animal care must be monitored as part of ensuring product safety. Personnel must be vaccinated with appropriate specific vaccines where necessary and must have regular health checks. Personnel whose health status could adversely affect product quality must be precluded from working in production areas.
rules.verbatim_quote: The health status of personnel should be taken into consideration as part of ensuring product safety. Where necessary, personnel engaged in production, maintenance, testing and animal care (and inspections) should be vaccinated with appropriate specific vaccines and have regular health checks. Any changes in the health status of personnel which could adversely affect the quality of the product should preclude their working in the production area, and appropriate records kept.
rules.exceptions: The scope and frequency of health monitoring should be commensurate with the risk to the product and personnel.

rules.rule_text: All personnel (including quality control, maintenance, cleaning and management staff) must not pass from areas with exposure to live microorganisms, genetically modified microorganisms, animal tissue, toxins, venoms or animals to areas where other products are handled, unless defined contamination control measures are followed.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, all personnel including those not routinely involved in the production operation (such as management, engineering staff and validation staff or auditors) should not pass from areas with exposure to live microorganisms, genetically modified microorganisms, animal tissue, toxins, venoms or animals to areas where other products (inactivated or sterile) or different organisms are handled. If such passage is unavoidable during a working day, then contamination control measures (for example, clearly defined decontamination measures such as a complete change of appropriate clothing and shoes, and showering if applicable) should be followed.
rules.exceptions: Contamination control measures may permit passage if defined and QRM-justified.

rules.rule_text: Staff assigned to BCG product production must not work with other infectious agents, must not work with virulent strains of Mycobacterium tuberculosis, must not be exposed to a known risk of tuberculosis infection, and must be carefully monitored with regular health checks screening for tuberculosis infection.
rules.verbatim_quote: Staff assigned to the production of BCG products should not work with other infectious agents. In particular, they should not work with virulent strains of Mycobacterium tuberculosis, nor should they be exposed to a known risk of tuberculosis infection (23). Additionally, they should be carefully monitored, with regular health checks that screen for tuberculosis infection.

rules.rule_text: The source, origin and suitability of all active substances, starting materials (including cryo-protectants, feeder cells, reagents, growth media, serum, enzymes, cytokines, growth factors and amino acids) and other components of the finished product must be clearly defined and controlled according to WHO GMP principles.
rules.verbatim_quote: The source, origin and suitability of active substances, starting materials (for example, cryo-protectants and feeder cells), buffers and media (for example, reagents, growth media, serum, enzymes, cytokines, growth factors and amino acids) and other components of the finished product should be clearly defined and controlled according to the principles set out in WHO guidance on GMP for pharmaceutical products (2).
cross_references: WHO GMP for pharmaceutical products TRS 986 Annex 2

rules.rule_text: Manufacturers must retain information describing the source and quality of biological materials used for at least 1 year after the expiry date of the finished products, and according to local regulations concerning biological products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturers should retain information describing the source and quality of the biological materials used for at least 1 year after the expiry date of the finished products and according to local regulations concerning biological products.
rules.exceptions: Documents retained for longer periods may provide useful information related to AEFIs and other investigations.

rules.rule_text: All starting material suppliers must be initially qualified on the basis of documented criteria and a risk-based approach. Regular assessments of supplier status must be conducted. Starting materials sourced from brokers must be carefully qualified, including by audit where needed.
rules.verbatim_quote: All starting material suppliers (that is, manufacturers) should be initially qualified on the basis of documented criteria and a risk-based approach. Regular assessments of their status should also be carried out... When starting materials are sourced from brokers who could increase the risk of contamination by performing repackaging operations under GMP (2, 4) they should be carefully qualified; an audit may form part of such qualification, as needed.

rules.rule_text: An identity test or equivalent must be performed on each batch of received starting materials prior to release. For starting materials used to manufacture active substances, the number of samples must be based on statistically recognized criteria and QRM principles. For starting materials and intermediates used in formulation of finished product, each container must be sampled for identity testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: An identity test, or equivalent, should be performed on each batch of received starting materials prior to release... for starting materials and intermediates used in the formulation of finished product each container should be sampled for identity testing in accordance with the main principles of GMP for pharmaceutical products unless reduced testing has been validated.

rules.rule_text: The risk of contamination of starting materials during their passage along the supply chain must be assessed, with particular emphasis on adventitious agents such as those causing TSEs. A quality risk assessment must be performed to evaluate the potential for adventitious agents in biological starting materials.
rules.verbatim_quote: The risk of contamination of starting materials during their passage along the supply chain should be assessed, with particular emphasis on adventitious agents such as those causing TSEs (24)... A quality risk assessment should be performed to evaluate the potential for adventitious agents in biological starting materials.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on TSEs in relation to biological and pharmaceutical products (2003)

rules.rule_text: The transport of critical materials, reference materials, active substances, human tissues and cells to the manufacturing site must be controlled as part of a written quality agreement between responsible parties if they are different commercial entities. Required traceability and cold chain requirements must be ensured, maintained and documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: The transport of critical materials, reference materials, active substances, human tissues and cells to the manufacturing site should be controlled as part of a written quality agreement between the responsible parties if they are different commercial entities. Manufacturing sites should have documentary evidence of adherence to the specified storage and transport conditions, including cold chain requirements, if required. The required traceability – starting at tissue establishments through to the recipient(s), and including the traceability of materials in contact with the cells or tissues – should be ensured, maintained and documented.

rules.rule_text: To prevent unwanted genetic drift, the production of biological products must be based on a system of master and working seed lots and/or cell banks. This seed lot/cell bank system is the beginning of the manufacturing process for certain biological products such as vaccines.
rules.source_section: Section 8.3 — Seed lots and cell banks
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to prevent the unwanted drift of genetic properties which might result from repeated subcultures or multiple generations, the production of biological products obtained by microbial culture, cell culture or propagation in embryos and animals should be based on a system of master and working seed lots and/or cell banks; which is the beginning of the manufacturing process of certain biological products (for example, vaccines).
rules.exceptions: Cell-based medicinal products may use a cell stock system as an alternative.

rules.rule_text: The number of generations (passages or doublings) between the seed lot or cell bank and the finished product, as defined as maximum in the marketing authorization dossier, must be consistent with that authorization and must not be exceeded.
rules.source_section: Section 8.4 — Seed lots and cell banks
rules.verbatim_quote: The number of generations (expressed as passages or doublings) between the seed lot or cell bank and the finished product, defined as maximum, should be consistent with the marketing authorization dossier and should not be exceeded.

rules.rule_text: Establishment and handling of MCBs and WCBs must be performed under demonstrably appropriate conditions in an appropriately controlled environment. No other living or infectious material should be handled simultaneously in the same area or by the same persons during establishment of seed lots and cell banks.
rules.source_section: Section 8.6 — Seed lots and cell banks
rules.verbatim_quote: Establishment and handling of the MCBs and WCBs should be performed under conditions which are demonstrably appropriate. These should include an appropriately controlled environment to protect the seed lot and the cell bank, and the personnel handling them... During the establishment of the seed lot and cell bank, no other living or infectious material (such as viruses, cell lines or microbial strains) should be handled simultaneously in the same area or by the same persons, as set out in current WHO Recommendations (26).
cross_references: WHO Recommendations for evaluation of animal cell cultures TRS 978 Annex 3

rules.rule_text: Quarantine and release procedures for master and working cell banks/seed lots must be followed, including adequate characterization and testing for contaminants. Initially, full characterization testing of the MCB must be done, including genetic identification. Viability, purity and stability-indicating attributes of seed lots and cell banks must be checked regularly according to justified criteria.
rules.source_section: Section 8.7 — Seed lots and cell banks
rules.verbatim_quote: Quarantine and release procedures for master and working cell banks/seed lots should be followed, including adequate characterization and testing for contaminants. Initially, full characterization testing of the MCB should be done, including genetic identification... Thereafter, the viability, purity and other stability-indicating attributes of seed lots and cell banks should be checked regularly according to justified criteria. Evidence of the stability and recovery of the seed lots and banks should be documented and records should be kept in a manner that permits trend evaluation.
rules.exceptions: A new MCB from a previous initial clone, MCB or WCB should be subjected to the same established testing as the original MCB, unless otherwise justified.

rules.rule_text: Each storage container for seed lots and cell banks must be adequately sealed, clearly labelled and kept at an appropriate temperature. Storage temperature must be recorded continuously and, where applicable, liquid nitrogen level monitored. Temperature deviations must be detected and recorded, with corrective and preventive action taken.
rules.source_section: Section 8.8 — Seed lots and cell banks
rules.verbatim_quote: Each storage container should be adequately sealed, clearly labelled and kept at an appropriate temperature. A stock inventory should be kept. The storage temperature should be recorded continuously and, where applicable, the liquid nitrogen level should be monitored. Any deviation from the set limits, and any corrective and preventive action taken, should be recorded. Temperature deviations should be detected as early as possible (for example, through the use of an alarm system for temperature and nitrogen levels).

rules.rule_text: MSLs and MCBs, and preferably also WSLs and WCBs, must be stored in two or more controlled separate sites in order to minimize the risk of total loss due to natural disaster, equipment malfunction or human error. A contingency plan must be in place.
rules.source_section: Section 8.10 — Seed lots and cell banks
rules.verbatim_quote: MSLs, MCBs, and preferably also WSLs and WCBs, should be stored in two or more controlled separate sites in order to minimize the risk of total loss due to natural disaster, equipment malfunction or human error. A contingency plan should be in place.
rules.exceptions: WSLs and WCBs storage at two separate sites is a preference, not a strict requirement.

rules.rule_text: Preparations containing live microorganisms or live viruses must not be manufactured and containers must not be filled in areas used for processing of other pharmaceutical products, unless effective containment and decontamination is validated.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, preparations containing live microorganisms or live viruses should not be manufactured and containers should not be filled in areas used for the processing of other pharmaceutical products. However, if the manufacturer can demonstrate and validate effective containment and decontamination of the live microorganisms and viruses then the use of multi-product facilities may be justifiable.
rules.exceptions: Multi-product facilities may be justified if effective containment and decontamination are demonstrated and validated using campaign production, closed systems, and/or disposable systems per QRM principles.

rules.rule_text: Validation studies must be carried out to ensure the effectiveness of cleaning, sanitization and disinfection, including elimination of residues of used agents. Cleaning and sanitization should take into account the growth-promoting agents used in biological product manufacture.
rules.verbatim_quote: Cleaning and sanitization should take into account the fact that processes often include the handling of growth media and other growth-promoting agents. Validation studies should be carried out to ensure the effectiveness of cleaning, sanitization and disinfection, including elimination of residues of used agents.

rules.rule_text: Airborne dissemination of live microorganisms and viruses used for the production process, including those from personnel, must be avoided.
rules.verbatim_quote: Airborne dissemination of live microorganisms and viruses used for the production process, including those from personnel, should be avoided.

rules.rule_text: Drainage systems must be designed such that effluents can be effectively neutralized or decontaminated to minimize the risk of cross-contamination. Specific and validated decontamination systems must be considered for effluents when infectious and/or potentially infectious materials are used. Local regulations must be complied with in order to minimize the risk of contamination of the external environment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Drainage systems should be designed in such a way that effluents can be effectively neutralized or decontaminated to minimize the risk of cross-contamination. Specific and validated decontamination systems should be considered for effluents when infectious and/or potentially infectious materials are used for production. Local regulations should be complied with in order to minimize the risk of contamination of the external environment according to the risk associated with the biohazardous nature of waste materials.

rules.rule_text: Dedicated production areas must be used for handling live cells capable of persistence in the manufacturing environment, for pathogenic organisms of Biosafety Risk Group 3 or 4, and/or for spore-forming organisms until inactivation is accomplished and verified. For Bacillus anthracis, Clostridium tetani and Clostridium botulinum, strictly dedicated facilities must be used for each individual product.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dedicated production areas should be used for the handling of live cells capable of persistence in the manufacturing environment, for pathogenic organisms of Biosafety Risk Group 3 or 4 and/or for spore-forming organisms until the inactivation process is accomplished and verified. For Bacillus anthracis, Clostridium tetani and Clostridium botulinum strictly dedicated facilities should be used for each individual product.
rules.exceptions: Campaign manufacture of spore-forming organisms may occur in a facility or suite of facilities where only one product is processed at any one time.

rules.rule_text: For Biosafety Risk Group 3 organisms, air must not be recirculated to any other area in the facility and must be exhausted through HEPA filters that are regularly checked for performance. For Biosafety Risk Group 4 organisms, a dedicated non-recirculating ventilation system and HEPA-filtering of exhaust air are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of Biosafety Risk Group 3 organisms, air should not be recirculated to any other area in the facility and should be exhausted through high-efficiency particulate air (HEPA) filters that are regularly checked for performance. A dedicated non-recirculating ventilation system and HEPA-filtering of exhaust air are required when handling Biosafety Risk Group 4 organisms (27).

rules.rule_text: Areas where Biosafety Risk Group 3 or 4 organisms are handled must always have a negative air pressure relative to the environment. Air-lock doors must be interlocked to prevent simultaneous opening. Differential pressure alarms must be present and validated and monitored.
rules.verbatim_quote: Areas where Biosafety Risk Group 3 or 4 organisms are handled should always have a negative air pressure relative to the environment. This will ensure the containment of the organism in unlikely events such as failure of the door interlock. Air-lock doors should be interlocked to prevent them being opened simultaneously. Differential pressure alarms should be present wherever required, and should be validated and monitored.

rules.rule_text: All critical biological processes including inoculation, multiplication, fermentation, cell disruption, inactivation, purification, virus removal, removal of toxic and harmful additives, filtration, formulation and aseptic filling are subject to process validation. Manufacturing control parameters to be validated may include specific addition sequences, mixing speeds, time and temperature controls, limits of light exposure and containment.
rules.verbatim_quote: All critical biological processes (including inoculation, multiplication, fermentation, cell disruption, inactivation, purification, virus removal, removal of toxic and harmful additives, filtration, formulation and aseptic filling) are subject, as applicable, to process validation. Manufacturing control parameters to be validated may include specific addition sequences, mixing speeds, time and temperature controls, limits of light exposure and containment.

rules.rule_text: Critical processes for inactivation or elimination of potentially harmful microorganisms of Biosafety Risk Group 2 or above, including genetically modified ones, are subject to validation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Critical processes for inactivation or elimination of potentially harmful microorganisms of Biosafety Risk Group 2 or above, including genetically modified ones, are subject to validation.

rules.rule_text: Penicillin and other beta-lactam antibiotics must not be used at any stage of biological product production. Antibiotics used during early stages of production to prevent inadvertent microbial contamination must be cleared from the manufacturing process at the stage specified in the marketing authorization, and acceptable residual levels must be defined and validated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Antibiotics may be used during the early stages of production to help prevent inadvertent microbial contamination or to reduce the bioburden of living tissues and cells. In this case, the use of antibiotics should be well justified, and they should be cleared from the manufacturing process at the stage specified in the marketing authorization. Acceptable residual levels should be defined and validated. Penicillin and other beta-lactam antibiotics should not be used at any stage of the process.

rules.rule_text: The reuse of purification components (e.g., resins, matrices, cassettes) for different products is not acceptable. Reuse of components at different stages of processing of one product, if performed, must be validated with defined acceptance criteria, operating conditions, regeneration methods, lifespan and sanitization/sterilization methods, cleaning process, and hold times.
rules.verbatim_quote: The reuse of components at different stages of processing of one product is discouraged but, if performed, should be validated... The reuse of components for different products is not acceptable.

rules.rule_text: The decision to use campaign manufacture must be justified in a documented manner based on a systematic risk approach for each product or strain, taking into account containment requirements and the risk of cross-contamination. Campaign changeover procedures including sensitive techniques for determination of residues must be validated. Proper cleaning acceptance criteria must be defined on a toxicology basis of product residues.
rules.verbatim_quote: The decision to use a facility or filling line for campaign manufacture should be justified in a documented manner and should be based on a systematic risk approach for each product (or strain) taking into account the containment requirements and the risk of cross-contamination to the next product. Campaign changeover procedures, including sensitive techniques used for the determination of residues, should be validated and proper cleaning acceptance criteria should be defined on a toxicology basis of product residues from the last campaign, as applicable.

rules.rule_text: The information provided on inner labels (container labels) and outer labels (packaging) of biological products must be readable and legible, and the content must be approved by the NRA. The suitability of labels for low and ultra-low storage temperatures must be verified where applicable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The information provided on the inner label (also called the container label) and on the outer label (on the packaging) should be readable and legible, and the content approved by the NRA... The suitability of labels for low and ultra-low storage temperatures should be verified, if applicable.

rules.rule_text: Reference samples of biological starting materials must be retained under recommended storage conditions for at least 1 year beyond the expiry date of the corresponding finished product. Reference samples of other starting materials and intermediates for which critical parameters cannot be tested in the final product must be retained for at least 2 years after the release of the product if stability allows.
rules.verbatim_quote: Reference samples of biological starting materials should be retained under the recommended storage conditions for at least 1 year beyond the expiry date of the corresponding finished product. Reference samples of other starting materials (other than solvents, gases and water) as well as intermediates for which critical parameters cannot be tested in the final product should be retained for at least 2 years after the release of the product if their stability allows for this storage period.
rules.exceptions: Certain starting materials such as components of growth media need not necessarily be retained.

rules.rule_text: Retention samples of finished biological products must be stored in their final packaging at the recommended storage conditions for at least 1 year after the expiry date.
rules.verbatim_quote: Retention samples of a finished product should be stored in their final packaging at the recommended storage conditions for at least 1 year after the expiry date.

rules.rule_text: All analytical methods used in the quality control and in-process control of biological products must be well characterized, validated and documented to a satisfactory standard. Fundamental validation parameters include linearity, accuracy, precision, selectivity/specificity, sensitivity and reproducibility.
rules.verbatim_quote: All analytical methods used in the quality control and in-process control of biological products should be well characterized, validated and documented to a satisfactory standard in order to yield reliable results. The fundamental parameters of this validation include linearity, accuracy, precision, selectivity/specificity, sensitivity and reproducibility.
rules.exceptions: For bioassays, additional measurements of performance of references, critical reagents and/or cell lines should also be considered during validation.

rules.rule_text: All stability studies of biological products must be carried out according to relevant WHO and other guidelines, including real-time/real-condition stability, accelerated stability and stress testing. Trend analysis of test results from the stability monitoring programme must assure early detection of any process or assay drift, and this information must be part of the product quality review (PQR).
rules.verbatim_quote: All stability studies – including real-time/real-condition stability, accelerated stability and stress testing – should be carried out according to relevant WHO and other guidelines (31) or other recognized documents. Trend analysis of the test results from the stability monitoring programme should assure the early detection of any process or assay drift, and this information should be part of the PQR of biological products.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines TRS 962 Annex 3

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing batch records of biological products must be retained for at least 1 year after the expiry date of the batch and must be readily retrievable for inspection by the NRA. In the case of vaccines, a batch processing record and a summary protocol must be prepared for each batch for the purpose of lot release by the NRA.
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturing batch records should be retained for at least 1 year after the expiry date of the batch of the biological product and should be readily retrievable for inspection by the NRA... In the case of vaccines, a batch processing record and a summary protocol should be prepared for each batch for the purpose of lot release by the NRA. The information included in the summary protocol should follow the WHO Guidelines for independent lot release of vaccines by regulatory authorities (36).
rules.exceptions: Documents retained for longer periods may provide useful information related to AEFIs and other investigations.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines for independent lot release of vaccines TRS 978 Annex 2

rules.rule_text: The presence of live animals in the production area must be avoided unless otherwise justified. Areas used for tests involving animals or microorganisms must be well separated from premises used for manufacturing of products and must have completely separate ventilation systems and separate staff.
rules.verbatim_quote: The presence of live animals in the production area should be avoided unless otherwise justified... Areas used for performing tests involving animals or microorganisms should be well separated from premises used for the manufacturing of products and should have completely separate ventilation systems and separate staff.
rules.exceptions: Embryonated eggs are allowed in the production area, if applicable.

rules.rule_text: A look-back procedure must be in place in relation to the decision-making process for evaluating the continued suitability of the biological active substance or finished product in which animal-sourced starting materials have been used or incorporated. This may include retesting of reference samples from previous collections from the same donor animal.
rules.verbatim_quote: A look-back procedure should be in place in relation to the decision-making process used to evaluate the continued suitability of the biological active substance or finished product in which animal-sourced starting materials have been used or incorporated. This decision-making process may include the retesting of reference samples from previous collections from the same donor animal (where applicable) to establish the last negative donation.

rules.rule_text: For cell-based products, microbiological tests (sterility tests or purity checks) must be conducted on cultures of cells or cell banks free of antibiotics and other inhibitory substances to provide evidence of absence of bacterial and fungal contamination. Where antibiotics are used, they must be removed by filtration at the time of testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: For cell-based products, microbiological tests (for example, sterility tests or purity checks) should be conducted on cultures of cells or cell banks free of antibiotics and other inhibitory substances in order to provide evidence of the absence of bacterial and fungal contamination, and to be able to detect fastidious organisms where appropriate. Where antibiotics are used, they should be removed by filtration at the time of testing.
